title: when tag IPv6 registry url, report "invalid reference format"
body:
**Description**

I want to push the image to a pure IPv6 registry. I use the rename image and find an error.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. A registry of IPv6 addresses
2. Use buildah tag busybox: buildah tag busybox '[2008::192:168:131:145]:30008/test/busybox:latest'


**Describe the results you received:**

`error adding names [[2008::192:168:131:145]:30008/test/busybox:latest] to image "busybox": error parsing name "[2008::192:168:131:145]:30008/test/busybox:latest": error parsing image name "[2008::192:168:131:145]:30008/test/busybox:latest": invalid reference format`


**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-1.11.6-11.el7_8.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.11.6
Go Version:      go1.12.12
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.1-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:
image Version:   5.0.0
Git Commit:
Built:           Thu Jan  1 08:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

I found a similar issue in docker, it seems that this is a general problem, does buildah plan to support IPv6 address resolution?

https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/39033

comments.body:
I think porting https://github.com/distribution/distribution/pull/3489 to `c/image` should fix this, but just curious why is `c/image` not using regexp directly from `distribution`.

 @mtrmac @vrothberg any idea about why `c/image` is not directly using `distribution` ?

Adding that syntax immediately directly conflicts with the `containers-storage:` syntax for `[various options]reference`.

So we can’t just start accepting that syntax without significant other work. (_Maybe_ we can write a parser that unambiguously decides whether `[…]` is an IPv6 address or options. I don’t know. Or maybe we need to invent some completely new syntax :/ .)

Also consider the risk of all the various hand-rolled parsers, in many shell scripts or possibly in the Podman codebase, just looking for `:` to get a tag or digest. I’ve been on a long crusade to replace substring searches with correct parsing, but I don’t have any illusions that I’ve been 100% successful even in just the Podman codebase, let alone outside code over which we have no control.

So, defining a new syntax that uses `:` for other purposes is, strictly speaking, always possible, but in practice fairly likely to break something. 

Pragmatically, as years go by without having support for raw IPv6 addresses, it gets easier to argue that adding support for raw IPv6 addresses is not _that_ necessary.

> Pragmatically, as years go by without having support for raw IPv6 addresses, it gets easier to argue that adding support for raw IPv6 addresses is not _that_ necessary.

when IPv4 addresses are exhausted, in the era when IPv6 is more popular, it is valuable to use the original IPv6 addresses to build a registry.

I think we are in a period of transition.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
